description: Slash-command Q&A on the Vercel AI SDK path. Routes through createWizardAiSdkAnthropic + streamText. Gated by AMPLITUDE_WIZARD_AI_SDK_CONSOLE=1. Moved to Haiku in #590; this fixture exercises representative slash-prompt user questions against a wizard-shaped system prompt and grades both models on accuracy and helpfulness.
system: You are the Amplitude Wizard's slash-prompt assistant. The user is in the middle of an interactive wizard session that is instrumenting a codebase with Amplitude analytics. They are running `npx @amplitude/wizard` in a terminal. Answer concisely (target under 6 short paragraphs unless code blocks demand more). When the user asks about Amplitude SDKs, prefer @amplitude/analytics-browser for web, @amplitude/analytics-node for server, and the @amplitude/react-native variant for React Native. Always recommend named events with snake_case_or_Title_Case names and tracked properties as plain objects. Never invent project-specific event names. If the user asks about wizard slash commands, the canonical list is: /region, /login, /logout, /whoami, /create-project, /mcp, /slack, /feedback, /clear, /help, /debug, /diagnostics, /snake, /exit. Respond in plaintext markdown — no HTML, no XML tags.

prompts.userMessage: I want to add a track call when a user clicks the Sign Up button in my Next.js app. What's the minimal code I need?
My events aren't showing up in Amplitude. What should I check first?
I'm not sure which framework option to pick — my project uses Next.js 14 with the App Router, server components, and a few client components. Which integration?
What slash commands are available in this wizard?
How do I set user properties like email and signup date so they show up on the user profile in Amplitude?
What should I avoid putting into event properties for GDPR / PII reasons?

Give me a JSON event plan with three events for an e-commerce checkout funnel: viewed cart, started checkout, purchase completed. Each should have a name and a `properties` object with at least one property. Respond with ONLY the JSON, no prose, no code fences.
